run.note: MEASURE (post-run, report-only): checks.post only asserts the mp4 + contact PNG are non-empty, never that the mp4 is a well-formed container with both tracks above a sane duration floor AND actually normalized, NOR that the contact PNG actually corresponds to THIS run's mp4 (make-contact-sheet.mjs's call site swallows its own errors, so a stale prior-run PNG can ship untouched). ffprobes the DELIVERABLE + independently re-measures its loudness (ffmpeg loudnorm, same I=-16/TP=-1 target render-complete-lesson.mjs itself aims for) + cross-checks the contact PNG's mtime against the mp4's own AND the sidecar `-contact.json` legend's totalDuration/fps against the mp4's measured duration, and writes a pass/fail-per-check report — fires on a corrupt, silent, truncated, un-normalized, or stale/mismatched-contact-sheet render that checks.post alone would miss.

measure.note: All 8 measure-render.mjs checks (exists/ffprobe-ran/has-video/has-audio/duration-floor/lufs-within-tolerance/contact-freshness/contact-duration-match) must pass; `failedChecks` in the report names which one didn't. contactFreshness+contactDurationMatch close the stale-contact-sheet hole: a PNG left over from a failed/skipped regen either has an older mtime than the freshly-rendered mp4, or its `-contact.json` legend's implied duration no longer matches the mp4's actual duration.

render-complete-lesson.mjs's 2-pass loudnorm block is wrapped in a try/catch that WARNS and continues on failure (never fails the node) — the mp4 then ships un-normalized with no artifact-level signal. The 'Loudnorm verify (re-measure)' step timing entry is only ever recorded after pass 1 + pass 2 + the file rename all succeeded, so its presence in render-timing.json is a reliable proxy that the deliverable was actually normalized. Coupled to the script's literal step labels (see memory.md) — re-sync if render-complete-lesson.mjs's loudnorm section is edited.
